MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jul-09-2015 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Jeffrey T. Thayer, of DEHAY ELLISTON LLP (510.285.0750), appearing for defendant /moving party Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.

Mahzad K. Hite, of LEVIN SIMES (415.426.3000), appearing for plaintiffs/opposing party Janet Whitaker Individually and on Behalf of the Estate of Asael Leon Whitaker, et al.


RULING:
DEFENDANT KAISER GYPSUM COMPANY, INC.'S NOTICE OF MOTION AND MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT.

Matter called, argument held. At the conclusion of argument, the Court adopted the tentative ruling: Defendant Kaiser Gypsum Company, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Whitaker was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. The Court did not need to consider the deposition testimony of George Kirk to find that defendant sustained its initial burden. Defendant's objections to plaintiffs' responses to interrogatories (Exhibit C) are sustained and its objections to its standard interrogatory responses (Exhibits E, H) are overruled. Defendant's objections nos. 4, 5, 6 and 7 are also overruled. Mr. Thayer, counsel for defendant/moving party, is to submit a proposed order pursuant to 3.1312 for the Court's consideration. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported. (503/TLJ)

MINI MINUTES:
No appearance and tentative ruling adopted: Defendant Volkswagen Group of America, Inc.'s demurrer to each cause of action in plaintiffs' complaint is sustained with ten days leave to amend to give plaintiffs an opportunity, if they are able to do so, to allege facts sufficient to meet the requirements of Bockrath v. Aldrich Chemical (1999) 21 Cal.4th 71. Defendant's special demurrer to the fourth cause of action (loss of consortium) is overruled. Plaintiffs' complaint sufficiently pleads a cause of action for loss of consortium and the authorities cited by defendant do not demonstrate that a cause of action for loss of consortium is precluded in a wrongful death action. Defendant's demurrer was timely filed. Prevailing party is to prepare an order in compliance with CRC 3.1312. Judge: Teri L. Jackson, Matter not reported = (503/TLJ)
